description: Perform a self-starting capability assessment for a 3-bladed H-Darrieus Vertical Axis Wind Turbine (VAWT) in QBlade. Design the rotor using a NACA 0021 airfoil with a polar generated at Reynolds number 300,000 for Angles of Attack from -10 to 20 degrees, extrapolated to a full 360-degree range. The rotor specifications are: 3.0 m blade height, 1.5 m rotor radius, 0.2 m blade chord, and 3 blades. Conduct a Double Multiple Streamtube (DMS) simulation at a wind speed of 8 m/s across a Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) range of 0.5 to 5.0. Determine if the turbine is self-starting based on whether there is positive torque at TSR < 1.0. Save the project to /home/ga/Documents/projects/vawt_selfstart.wpa and create a report at /home/ga/Documents/projects/vawt_selfstart_report.txt containing the self-starting assessment (YES/NO), lowest positive torque TSR, optimal TSR, maximum Cp, and calculated rotor solidity (N*c / (2*pi*R)).
